title: Prawn cocktail
subtitle: Cold prawns, Marie Rose sauce, crisp lettuce
excerpt: Cooked cold prawns on shredded iceberg or little gem lettuce, dressed with Marie Rose sauce, the mayonnaise, ketchup, and Worcestershire sauce combination that defines the dish. A slice of lemon and a dusting of paprika complete it. The British 1970s starter that never actually went away.
sourceNotes: Prawn cocktail became the defining British restaurant starter of the 1970s, so associated with that era that it acquired an ironic reputation for decades. The dish is older. It appears in American cookbooks from the 1920s as 'shrimp cocktail'. The British version with Marie Rose sauce and iceberg lettuce was popularised by Fanny Cradock in the 1960s and became ubiquitous throughout the 70s and 80s.

content.text: The Marie Rose sauce should be made with good mayonnaise, not salad cream. The balance of ketchup to mayonnaise is a matter of taste, start with a 3:1 ratio and adjust. The sauce should be pink-orange, thick enough to coat the prawns without being a solid paste.

Mix the {{mayonnaise}}, {{ketchup}}, {{worcestershire-sauce}}, and lemon juice together to make the Marie Rose sauce. Taste and adjust, add more ketchup for sweetness, more lemon for sharpness. Toss the {{prawns-cooked}} in half the sauce. Divide the shredded {{baby-gem}} between glasses or bowls. Spoon the dressed prawns on top. Add a small drizzle of the remaining sauce over the top. Dust lightly with {{paprika-sweet}}. Serve with {{lemon}} wedges and thinly sliced brown bread and butter alongside.

items.fix: Drain cooked prawns thoroughly and pat dry with kitchen paper before adding to the sauce. Frozen-defrosted prawns release more water, squeeze them gently in a cloth
items.cause: Prawns not drained or patted dry
items.symptom: Prawns watery and diluting the sauce

items.fix: Add acid and ketchup gradually. The sauce should coat a spoon, add another tablespoon of mayonnaise if too thin
items.cause: Too much lemon juice or ketchup added
items.symptom: Sauce too thin and running to the bottom

content.text: Prawn cocktail was the British dinner party starter of the 1970s and 80s. Its period association gave it a dated reputation through the 1990s that has fully lifted. It appears now on upmarket restaurant menus served with good prawns and freshly made sauce. At home it is a fast, reliable first course for a dinner party.
